preamble: Epenthesis of [u] serves to break up difficult consonant clusters, providing a vowel nucleus where phonotactics require one. It is well-documented in Semitic and Berber languages where underlying consonant sequences must be split by a vowel in certain morphological contexts.

examples.note: Insertion of /u/ to break up consonant clusters in Arabic morphology; the vowel quality is determined by morphological template (kataba, kutiba, kutub etc.).
Epenthetic /u/ appears near labialized consonants or at word edges to satisfy phonotactic constraints.
